title: Add support for 'podman top' on FreeBSD
body:
This is a simplistic implementation which runs ps(1) on the host and does not try to support 'podman pod top' - I'll tackle that in a separate PS.

```release-note
None
```

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *dfr*, *mheon* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [mheon] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
Question, how do plan to document this? https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/docs/source/markdown/podman-top.1.md mentions descriptors that are not usable for on bsd.

> Question, how do plan to document this? https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/docs/source/markdown/podman-top.1.md mentions descriptors that are not usable for on bsd.

Thats a good question - I am hoping to be able to use markdown-preprocess in some way to help with this, perhaps extending @@option and/or @@include to allow platform-specific includes. This could also be used to strip out linux-only flags (e.g. cgroup related) from the FreeBSD manpages.

In the short term, 'podman top --help' does list the set of supported descriptors - I will probably expand that list to include the full list from FreeBSD's ps(1) manpage.

I think the options/include is a good idea.  Perhaps support a .ARCH flag. For example
```
device-read-bps.md
device-read-bps.md.freebsd
```
On all non freebsd platforms, it reads the default.  On freebsd it reads the specific version.  If file is empty then the option does not get added.

Similarly we could handle this with podman-remote.1.md versus podman.1.md
```
device-read-bps.md
device-read-bps.md.freebsd
device-read-bps.md.remote
```


@edsantiago WDYT?

My gut reaction is a literal "oof", like a punch in the gut. I see that as introducing high complexity and high risk for very little benefit. Couldn't we just add a human-readable "on FreeBSD, this-and-that" note to the existing documents?

> My gut reaction is a literal "oof", like a punch in the gut. I see that as introducing high complexity and high risk for very little benefit. Couldn't we just add a human-readable "on FreeBSD, this-and-that" note to the existing documents?

I certainly don't want to add friction or risk for this. For now, I'm not focusing on the manpages at all while I am working through adding functionality but when I return to it, I'll try to keep things as simple as possible.

I'm going to go ahead and merge this, given we're deferring manpage work.

/lgtm
